_comment: Regression for the wrappedModuleTreeshaking external-reexport-barrel guard (proposal §3 flag-on sweep). `barrel.js` is a side-effect-free ESM barrel that re-exports a binding from an EXTERNAL module (node:path); main reads it. Under the obligation model the skip would normally prune a side-effect-free barrel — but pruning THIS one would drop the external import the barrel owns (the canonical owner is the external module, not WrapKind::Esm, so the finalizer's canonical-follow cannot re-emit it), leaving `sep` resolving to nothing / an ambient global. The fix: never skip a barrel that directly imports from an external. Both the base strictExecutionOrder variant and the [wrapped_module_treeshaking: true] variant MUST keep the `node:path` import and `sep` (snapshots identical); execution asserts `typeof sep === 'string'`, so a regression that drops the external also fails at runtime.
